title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description:
Given a sorted array that has been rotated at some unknown pivot (e.g. `[4,5,6,7,0,1,2]` was originally `[0,1,2,4,5,6,7]`), search for a target. Return its index, or -1 if not found.

Must run in **O(log n)**.

**The point**: binary search is more flexible than 'array is sorted ascending'. As long as you can decide which half contains the target, you can shrink the window by half each step.
hints: Linear search would be O(n). The constraint says O(log n) — so binary search with a twist.
After rotation, at least one of [lo..mid] or [mid..hi] is still in sorted order. Figure out which.
If the left half is sorted (nums[lo] ≤ nums[mid]) and target is in [nums[lo], nums[mid]), go left. Otherwise go right. Mirror logic if the right half is sorted.
